document.source_title: Trump delays ‘scheduled attack’ on Iran, crediting ‘serious negotiations’

claims.claim_text: Trump will postpone a scheduled attack against Iran.
claims.evidence_text: United States President Donald Trump has announced he will postpone a “scheduled attack” against Iran at the request of regional leaders in the Middle East.
The reversal, he said, came in light of the fact that “serious negotiations are now taking place”.

entities.evidence_text: United States President Donald Trump has announced he will postpone a 'scheduled attack' against Iran.

entities.name: Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani
entities.evidence_text: Trump credited the intervention of leaders including Qatari Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani.

entities.name: Mohammed bin Salman
entities.evidence_text: Trump credited the intervention of leaders including Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.

entities.name: Pete Hegseth
entities.evidence_text: I have instructed Secretary of War, Pete Hegseth.

entities.evidence_text: The Chairman of The Joint Chiefs of Staff, General Daniel Caine.

entities.name: Masoud Pezeshkian
entities.evidence_text: Earlier on Monday, Iran’s President Masoud Pezeshkian wrote on social media.

entities.description: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense.
entities.evidence_text: The war has cost the country at least $29bn so far, according to Pentagon officials.

events.event: Ceasefire deal struck between Iran and the US.
events.evidence_text: Iran and the US struck a ceasefire deal on April 8.
Pakistan has been acting as a mediator since the US joined Israel in attacking Iran on February 28.

relationships.object: release of frozen assets and lifting of sanctions
